OFFSITE WORKSHOP · JUNE 16, 2026

Community —
the path to 1M MAU.

60 minutes.
WARMUP · SILENT · 3 MINUTES

Take a moment to reflect.

1

Which social product do you spend the most time on? When do you open it, and why does it feel valuable in the moment?

2

Now think of one that never took off. Why did it stall, even with every advantage?

e.g. Clubhouse, Threads, BeReal, Google+

3:00

Expectations, assumptions, and what we believe.

We're not debating form factor or philosophy today — we're here for the strategic calls that get us our first 1,000 people who love this.

FIRST PRINCIPLES

Why should a Leland community exist in the world?

For the customer?

What do they get from a Leland community that they can't get anywhere else?

For the business?

How does this compound everything else we're building, and fuel the core?

Why now?

If we delayed, what would we miss about our customers? If we nail it, how does it amplify the core?

To reach 1M, we must build something 1,000 people love.

Today's focus
0 → 1K
Find the people who genuinely love it.
1K → 100K
Make it spread.
100K → 1M
Make it inevitable.

Three different games. Mass scale starts with knowing exactly where we begin — and what we have to answer first.

§02 · 10 MINUTES

Breakouts.

6 groups → 3 on Users, 3 on Experts.
Users · Groups 1, 3, 5 Experts · Groups 2, 4, 6 We regroup as a room after.
10:00

The discussion board.

Leave it up — reference it while you dig in.
Theme 1 · Users · Groups 1, 3, 5
Who exactly is our initial ICP? Who is not? How narrow can we get?
Why will someone open Leland's community over LinkedIn, Instagram, X, or YouTube — when they're feeling motivated? when they're tired, bored, stressed, or procrastinating?
What's the trigger that turns it into a habit — when and why do they keep coming back?
What will drive our share loop / k-factor? Why specifically will someone send this to a friend or drop it in a group chat? What unique elements could drive a growth loop? (e.g., Substack → email) How can livestream traffic and our existing customer base seed the demand side and increase our shots on goal?
SpicyWho will not be a daily/weekly active user?
SpicyWhat's the bar — good, great, excellent? How feature-rich and polished before users keep coming back?
Theme 2 · Experts · Groups 2, 4, 6
Which experts will be most motivated to engage in community? Be specific on persona.
Why will experts post on day 1? Why will they keep posting after day 30? If users aren't engaging with content, what keeps experts motivated?
Content quantity: how do we ensure there's enough — and keep it flowing?
Content quality: how do we keep it useful? What makes it uniquely valuable?
SpicyAllow self-promotion vs. restrict it? Pros, cons, and risks.
SpicyDo we need synthetic content to escape cold-start — or what would have to be true to win without it?
Theme 1 · Users — regroup as a room · 15 min

Let's converge on the Users side.

Who is our initial ICP — and who is not? How narrow can we get?
Why open Leland's community over LinkedIn, Instagram, X, or YouTube — when motivated, and when tired or bored?
The habit trigger — when and why do they keep coming back?
The share loop / k-factor — why send it to a friend, and what unique elements drive growth? (e.g., Substack → email)
SpicyWho will not be a daily/weekly active user?
SpicyWhat's the bar — good, great, excellent — and how polished before users keep coming back?
Theme 2 · Experts — regroup as a room · 15 min

Now the Experts side.

Which expert persona is most motivated to engage in community? Be specific.
Why post on day 1 — and why keep posting after day 30? If users aren't engaging, what keeps experts motivated?
Content quantity — how do we keep enough flowing?
Content quality — what makes it uniquely valuable?
SpicyAllow self-promotion or restrict it? Pros, cons, risks.
SpicyDo we need synthetic content to escape cold-start — or what would have to be true to win without it?
SECTION 03

How do we approach success?

THE BIG DEBATE

Iterate rapidly.

Ship, learn, and refine fast — keep attempting until we find product–market fit.

Nail initial momentum.

Craft the first launch with care — priming both sides so momentum builds the moment we go live.

vs
PRE-MORTEM · SILENT 60 SECONDS, THEN DISCUSS

It's 12 months
from now.

Community is struggling.

It never found product–market fit. List every way it failed.

Community is thriving.

It's accelerating and people love it. Picture the future state — why?
